name: Couscous with Spicy Chick Pea Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 1/3 cups Cooked Dried Chick Peas (or 2 cans of 14-ounce chick peas, drained)
recipeInstructions:
1. Spread the Couscous out on a large deep plate and pour the warm water evenly over it. Rub the couscous lightly with your fingers to separate the grains. Set the plate aside.
2. Heat half the oil in a large saucepan, add the onions and carrots, and sauté gently for 10 minutes. Stir in the spices and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, stirring.
3. Stir in the corn, zucchini, and chick peas (or fava beans). Add the water and tomato paste. Bring to a boil, then turn down the heat so that the chick pea stew simmers.
4. By now, the couscous will have absorbed the water. Put it into a metal sieve, colander, or steamer saucepan, breaking it up gently with your fingers as you do so. If your container has large holes, you may prefer to line it with a piece of cheesecloth to prevent the grains from falling through, but this is usually unnecessary.
5. Place the steamer, colander, or sieve over the vegetable stew and cover with a lid or plate. Cook gently for 25-30 minutes.
6. Season to taste with black pepper. Stir in the remaining olive oil into the couscous. Transfer the couscous to a large, warmed serving dish, garnish with coriander, and serve immediately. Serve with a green salad.
